description_long: This object is a detailed representation of a spiny lobster, featuring a segmented, elongated body with a hard exoskeleton covered in small spines and bumps. The cephalothorax is robust and armored, with two long, whip-like antennae extending forward and slightly upward. The abdomen is clearly segmented, tapering towards a broad, fan-shaped tail. Multiple walking legs are visible, each jointed and covered in fine spines, while the absence of large claws distinguishes it from true lobsters. The coloration is a mix of earthy browns, reddish hues, and subtle greenish tints, with lighter spots and banding along the body and legs. The eyes are prominent and set on short stalks at the front of the head, providing a wide field of vision.
description: A spiny lobster with a segmented, armored body, long antennae, and multiple spiny legs, colored in browns and reddish hues with a fan-shaped tail.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the body, long antennae, segmented tail, and spiny legs.
description_view_1: Rear view highlighting the broad, fan-shaped tail and symmetrical arrangement of the legs.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the spiny exoskeleton, long antennae, and detailed segmentation of the abdomen.
description_view_3: Frontal view emphasizing the head, forward-projecting antennae, and the placement of the eyes on short stalks.
